commentary: 200A service upgrade wrapped in Fishtown today. Old fuse box from the fifties out, new panel in, PECO coordinated the meter swap. Clean work.
Apprentices: learn to bend conduit by hand before you touch a bender. You'll respect the trade more and your offsets will be cleaner.
Spring honey extraction starting next month. Twelve colonies came through winter strong. Wildflower this year tastes like the lindens did well.
Twenty years licensed this spring. Still learning something on every job. That's the trade.
PSA: GFCI protection is required on basement and garage circuits. If your panel predates it, get it looked at. Cheap insurance.
Built a run of deep frames over the weekend. Cedar, no glue, just nails and patience. Bees don't care, but I do.
Renewed my PA Master Electrician license. Code changes every cycle. If you're not reading the updates, you're behind.
Cold snap means service calls. Space heaters on old circuits trip breakers, sometimes worse. Don't run a 1500W heater off a lamp cord.
Wrapped the year. Honey sold out by November, electric work steady. Quiet season now for bookkeeping and frame building.
Wholesale honey out to Cedarbrook General and the Ridgemont Co-op this week. Last cases of the wildflower.
If a contractor quotes a panel upgrade without pulling a permit, walk away. Permit and inspection protect you, not them.
Knob-and-tube in a Fox Chase row house today. Careful work, no shortcuts. These old homes deserve respect.
Fall colony inspection done. Mites under control, stores look good for winter. Wrapped the hives this weekend.
Looking for a reliable second-year apprentice. Philadelphia metro, residential and light commercial. Show up, work hard, ask questions.
Saturday at Briarwood Market. Honey, a few cutting boards I turned this winter. Last outdoor market of the season.
Three estimates this week, all panel work. Older neighborhoods catching up on electrical. Good for the trade, good for safety.
Reminder: aluminum branch wiring from the sixties and seventies needs proper connectors. AlumiConn or a pro. Don't ignore it.
Built a couple cutting boards from walnut offcuts. Same hands that pull wire all week. Good to make something quiet.
Estimated tax filed, books closed with Steve. Slow January is for paperwork. Honey and electric both clean on the year.
EV charger install in Glenside. Level 2, dedicated 50A circuit. More of these every month. The grid's going to feel it.
First warm day. Bees flying, did a quick check. Queen laying, brood looks good. Spring's close.
Old timer taught me: measure the load, not the breaker. People oversize panels and undersize the work. Do it right.
